cards.title: Trump Administration Cracks Down on Anthropic
cards.body: The Trump administration has taken action against Anthropic, prompting discussion about what triggered these moves. The implications could reshape the broader AI ecosystem and competitive landscape.

cards.title: iOS 27 Brings Practical AI Features Beyond Siri
cards.body: Apple's iOS 27 includes AI enhancements throughout the OS beyond the Siri overhaul announced at WWDC. These features focus on practical utility across multiple Apple applications.

cards.title: Signal's Whittaker: AI Chatbots Aren't Friends
cards.body: Meredith Whittaker warns users that AI chatbots lack consciousness and sentience. She emphasizes these tools should not be treated as conscious beings or true interlocutors.

cards.title: DeepMind Nobel Laureate Joins Anthropic
cards.body: John Jumper, a Nobel laureate at Google DeepMind, is leaving to join rival Anthropic. His departure follows other notable exits from DeepMind.

cards.title: Export Controls Failed to Stop Cybersecurity Software
cards.body: A 30-year history shows export controls on cybersecurity software have proven ineffective. The effectiveness of similar restrictions on Anthropic's Mythos model remains questionable.

cards.title: US Bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5
cards.body: The US government forced Anthropic to pull Fable 5 and Mythos 5 over national security concerns following alleged guardrail bypass discoveries by Amazon researchers. The ban could inadvertently boost Anthropic's brand visibility.

cards.title: Reliance Weaves AI Into Telecom Services
cards.body: Billionaire Ambani's Reliance is integrating AI across calls, apps, and home services for over 500 million users. The move represents massive-scale AI deployment in telecom infrastructure.

cards.title: Elastic Acquires Deductive AI for $85M
cards.body: Elastic has agreed to buy CRV-backed Deductive AI, which uses AI to identify and resolve software bugs. The acquisition values the three-year-old startup at up to $85 million.

cards.title: Inference Startup Baseten Raises $1.5B
cards.body: AI inference startup Baseten is reportedly closing a $1.5 billion funding round at a $13 billion valuation. The deal reflects ongoing investor enthusiasm for the inference sector.

cards.title: Snap Spins Off AI Video Team as Dotmo
cards.body: Snapchat is spinning off its AI video team into a new company called Dotmo to manage costs. Current Snap staff leading the division will transition to the independent venture.

cards.title: OpenAI Recruits Top Talent Ahead of IPO
cards.body: OpenAI is hiring high-profile executives including Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball. The moves signal preparations for the company's planned IPO.

cards.title: US Singles Skeptical About Dating App AI
cards.body: Nearly 47% of US singles view AI use in dating negatively, according to Match data. However, many users welcome AI assistance for profile improvements and conversation starters.

cards.title: Amazon Challenges Nvidia with AI Chip Sales
cards.body: AWS is negotiating to sell its custom AI chips to competing data centers. CEO Andy Jassy estimates this represents a $50 billion opportunity for Amazon.

cards.title: FERC Gives Data Centers Grid Fast Lane
cards.body: Federal regulators ordered grid operators to prioritize data center interconnections for speed. However, the mandate does not address underlying electricity supply shortages.

cards.title: How to Disable AI in Google Docs
cards.body: Google Docs users can turn off the "Write with Gemini" feature to remove AI pop-ups. The guide provides steps to disable AI assistance in the application.

cards.title: Beyond Siri: Practical AI Features Coming to iOS 27
cards.body: Apple's iOS 27 brings more than Siri's AI overhaul, with useful AI features rolling out across the operating system. The updates focus on practical, everyday functionality for iPhone users.

cards.title: Signal's Meredith Whittaker on AI Chatbot Reality
cards.body: Whittaker emphasizes that AI chatbots are not conscious beings or sentient interlocutors, challenging user misconceptions. Her message underscores the importance of understanding AI's actual capabilities and limitations.

cards.title: Nobel Laureate John Jumper Joins Anthropic from DeepMind
cards.body: Jumper, a prominent AI researcher, is leaving Google DeepMind for rival Anthropic. His departure reflects ongoing talent movement within the competitive AI industry.

cards.title: US Government Bans Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5
cards.body: The US government forced Anthropic to pull two newest AI models citing national security concerns after researchers allegedly found guardrail bypass methods. The ban raises questions about AI governance and export controls.

cards.title: Deductive AI Caught by Elastic in $85M Acquisition
cards.body: Deductive AI, an AI startup that catches and resolves software bugs, was founded just three years ago. Elastic's acquisition signals growing demand for AI-powered debugging solutions.

cards.title: Baseten Raises $1.5B as Inference Gold Rush Continues
cards.body: AI inference startup Baseten is finalizing a $1.5 billion funding round at a $13 billion valuation. The mega-round reflects investor appetite for AI inference infrastructure.

cards.title: Snap Spins Off AI Video Team into Dotmo
cards.body: Snapchat is spinning off an internal AI video development unit into a new company called Dotmo. The move reflects the high costs and strategic importance of AI video technology.

cards.title: OpenAI Bolsters Leadership Ahead of IPO
cards.body: OpenAI hired Noam Shazeer, a Transformer co-inventor from Google DeepMind, and Dean Ball, a former Trump AI policy official. The hires signal OpenAI's preparation for its upcoming public listing.

cards.title: Amazon Challenges Nvidia by Selling AI Chips
cards.body: AWS is in talks to sell its AI chips to other data centers, with CEO Andy Jassy calling it a $50 billion opportunity. The move could reshape the AI infrastructure market.

cards.title: How to Turn Off AI in Google Docs
cards.body: Google's AI features in Docs can be disabled by users annoyed with "write with Gemini" pop-ups. A quick guide helps users regain control over their document experience.

cards.title: Subquadratic Claims Major LLM Bottleneck Breakthrough
cards.body: Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic claims to have solved a mathematical bottleneck holding back large language models for nearly a decade. Though details remain thin, the announcement has generated significant industry attention.

cards.title: Brain-Computer Interface Trials Accelerating Rapidly
cards.body: Brain-computer interface technology is advancing, with users like Casey Harrell utilizing implants for nearly three years. The devices enable paralyzed individuals to communicate and interact with technology.
